subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Expanding Universe
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Expanding Universe.

hook.text: In 1929, Edwin Hubble discovered that galaxies are flying apart. The farther away, the faster they recede.

buildup.text: He measured redshift—light stretched to longer wavelengths by the expansion of space—in dozens of galaxies.

discovery.text: This meant the universe is expanding. Run the clock backward and everything converges to a single point.

twist.text: Einstein had added a term to keep his equations static. He called it his 'biggest blunder' after Hubble proved him wrong.

climax.text: The Hubble constant measures the expansion rate. Pinning down its exact value remains one of cosmology's biggest debates.

punchline.text: Galaxies racing apart. The universe has been growing since birth.

quiz.question: What did Hubble's observations of galaxy redshifts reveal?
options: The universe is expanding, with galaxies moving apart from each other
Galaxies are rotating around a central point
Light from distant galaxies is being absorbed by dust
